LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER KGO TELEVISION, INC.'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE DIRECTED TO RESPONDENTS ORDERING COMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT AND SAN FRANCISCO'S SUNSHINE ORDINANCE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT RELIES ON ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE IN DENYING THE WRIT OF MANDATE. RELEASING P.S.R.'S IN UNREDACTED FORM WILL NOT GIVE THE PUBLIC A CLEARER UNDERSTANDING OF HOW THE M.T.A. CONDUCTS THE PUBLIC'S BUSINESS AND WOULD CONSTITUTE INVASION OF PRIVACY. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED RAISES MATERIAL QUESTIONS OF FACT AS TO THE RELIABILITY OF THE P.S.R.'S, ABSENT A FINDING OF AN ACTUAL VIOLATION. THE P.S.R.'S ARE TO BE RELEASED WITH DRIVER IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION REDACTED. THE P.S.R.'S ARE TO BE SEGREGATED BY DRIVER AND INCLUDE ANY ACTION TAKEN BY THE M.T.A. IN RESPONSE TO THE P.S.R. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR 7433

Off Calendar Mar-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER KGO TELEVISION, INC.'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE DIRECTED TO RESPONDENTS ORDERING COMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT AND SAN FRANCISCO'S SUNSHINE ORDINANCE.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL D. NELSON, ESQUIRE (NEVIN & ABSALOM, 415. 392.5040) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF TRANSPORT WORKERS' UNION 250A. JONATHAN ROLNICK, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3815) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY. THOMAS R. BURKE, ESQUIRE (DAVIS WRIGHT TREMAINE, 415.276.6552) APPEARING FOR KGO.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT RELIES ON ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE IN DENYING THE WRIT OF MANDATE. RELEASING P.S.R.'S IN UNREDACTED FORM WILL NOT GIVE THE PUBLIC A CLEARER UNDERSTANDING OF HOW THE M.T.A. CONDUCTS THE PUBLIC'S BUSINESS AND WOULD CONSTITUTE INVASION OF PRIVACY. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED RAISES MATERIAL QUESTIONS OF FACT AS TO THE RELIABILITY OF THE P.S.R.'S, ABSENT A FINDING OF AN ACTUAL VIOLATION. THE P.S.R.'S ARE TO BE RELEASED WITH DRIVER IDENTIFICATION INFORMATION REDACTED. THE P.S.R.'S ARE TO BE SEGREGATED BY DRIVER AND INCLUDE ANY ACTION TAKEN BY THE M.T.A. IN RESPONSE TO THE P.S.R. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR 7433
